domain: Korean kimchi multi-stage fermentation (4-stage biological process: brining selection + warm ferment Leuconostoc + mid ferment Lactobacillus + cold storage psychrotrophic LAB; each stage characterized by temperature + microbial community + metabolic targets)
specific_mechanism: Sequential biological stages with distinct microbial communities operating at distinct temperatures + metabolic outcomes; community shift at each phase boundary.
llm_application: KIMCHI-PIPELINE: a 4-stage temperature-controlled LLM training pipeline. Stage 1 PRE-FILTER (data filter + dedup + quality threshold, 'brining'). Stage 2 BROAD-PRETRAIN (broad mix, high LR, 'warm ferment'). Stage 3 DOMAIN-FINE-TUNE (specialized domain, med LR, 'mid ferment'). Stage 4 ALIGN-COOL (RLHF/SFT, low LR, 'cold storage'). Each stage = (data distribution D_k, LR schedule LR_k, duration T_k). Explicit DISTRIBUTION SHIFTS at boundaries; each phase optimised for one functional axis. Differs from mid-training survey by COUPLED data-distribution + LR + duration phase boundary formalism and 4-stage explicit framing.
novelty_claim: Mid-training survey (2510.06826) describes annealing-style phases refining data quality + optimization + context length. CAMPUS curriculum instruction tuning maintains sub-curricula. Multi-stage continual FT preserves capabilities. KIMCHI-PIPELINE differs by (a) 4 SEQUENTIAL STAGES with COUPLED data + LR + duration, (b) EXPLICIT DISTRIBUTION SHIFT at boundary, (c) microbial-community-analog distribution-shift formalism. Marginal novelty over existing annealing-mid-training.
